attacking_style: Opportunistic breakaway rider who survives medium difficulty climbs through smart positioning rather than raw climbing power, then executes sprints from small groups.
team_tactics: Limited data, but showed ability to operate independently in breakaways without visible team support for final execution.
terrain_preference: Breakaway-friendly stages with climbs difficult enough to thin the peloton but not steep enough to drop competent riders. Excels in reduced sprint scenarios from 5-10 riders.
vulnerability: Lacks the pure climbing strength to stay with elite climbers on sustained/steep ascents. Relies on others to control race before sprint finish.
key_patterns: Wins from small breakaway sprints rather than solo efforts or bunch kicks.
Survives climbs through tactical positioning despite not being strongest climber present.
Targets stages where breakaway survival probability high, then out-sprints survivors.
form_trajectory: unknown: only single race observation from 2017, insufficient data for trajectory assessment
